Class ObjectVariable<T>

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.variable.ObjectVariable<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the object
All Implemented Interfaces:
Variable<T>
public class ObjectVariable<T> extends Object implements Variable<T>
A variable storing an object. This can be also used to create a list variable, e.g.: 
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

Variable<List<String>> listVar = new ObjectVariable<>(new ArrayList<>());
// do something with the list
listVar.getValue().add("Example value");
// ...

Note: It is discouraged to use this class as a primitive variable because of the (un)boxing overhead. See other implementations of Variable for that purpose.

    Description
    ObjectVariable(Class<T> type)
    Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the initial value null.
    ObjectVariable(Class<T> type, T initialValue)
    Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the given initial value.
    ObjectVariable(T initialValue)
    Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the given initial value.

  • Constructor Details

    • ObjectVariable

      public ObjectVariable(Class<T> type)
      Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the initial value null.

      Note: If you want to call the ObjectVariable(Object) constructor with a Class argument, explicitly specify the generic parameter as Class. Example: 

      new ObjectVariable<Class<?>>(String.class)
      Parameters:
      type - the runtime type of this variable (required due to type erasure)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

    • ObjectVariable

      public ObjectVariable(T initialValue)
      Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the given initial value. The type is inferred from the initial value's runtime type, so the value must not be null!
      Parameters:
      initialValue - the initial value (should not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if initial value is null

    • ObjectVariable

      public ObjectVariable(Class<T> type, T initialValue)
      Constructs a new ObjectVariable with the given initial value.
      Parameters:
      type - the runtime type of this variable
      initialValue - the initial value (nullable)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

  • Method Details

    • getValue

      public T getValue()
      Returns the current value of this variable.
      Specified by:
      getValue in interface Variable<T>
      Returns:
      the value

    • setValue

      public void setValue(T newValue)
      Sets the value of this variable to the given one.
      Specified by:
      setValue in interface Variable<T>
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new value

    • is

      public boolean is(Class<?> cls)
      Returns whether the current value of this variable can be exactly represented as the class represented by the given Class object. Primitive classes should also be accepted.
      Specified by:
      is in interface Variable<T>
      Parameters:
      cls - the class
      Returns:
      true if the current value is an instance of the given class, false otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given class object is null (optional)

    • copy

      public ObjectVariable<T> copy()
      Creates and returns a shallow copy of this variable. The copy should not reflect changes to the source Variable, however, the value's state may be shared.
      Specified by:
      copy in interface Variable<T>
      Returns:
      a copy of this variable

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the current value.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of the current value
